compiled_state.title: Perf regression RED test suite: per-op hyperfine runner against A0 baseline
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Task

Write `tests/scripts/test-ticket-perf-regression.sh` — the per-op latency regression test runner.

### Test Logic

1. Guard: if hyperfine not available, print SKIP and exit 0 (CI jobs that install hyperfine will catch regressions; dev machines without it should not block)
2. Guard: if `tests/perf/a0-baseline.json` does not exist, exit 1 with message "A0 baseline not found — run tests/scripts/capture-a0-baseline.sh first" (this makes the test RED before Task b48d exists)
3. For each covered op (show, list, comment, create, tag, untag, edit, transition), run:
   ```bash
   hyperfine --runs 20 --export-json /tmp/perf-$op.json ".claude/scripts/dso ticket $op ..."
   ```
4. Compare median from hyperfine JSON vs a0-baseline.json entry for the op; fail if median > a0_median * 1.10 (>10% regression)
5. Print per-op pass/fail table; exit 1 if any op regressed

### Testing Mode

RED — exits 1 before a0-baseline.json exists.

## File Impact

- tests/scripts/test-ticket-perf-regression.sh (CREATE)
- .test-index (UPDATE)

## Acceptance

- exits non-zero before a0-baseline.json exists
- chmod +x set
